text.title: immersive-design-principles Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for immersive-design-principles as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mixing head-locked UI (crosshairs, fixed menus) with world-locked objects (moving enemies, environmental geometry) in the same viewport.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Conflicts in reference frame (head vs. world) create vestibular conflict. Users experience disorientation and motion sickness.

failure-mode.description: Comfort guidelines are population averages; ~10% of users get sick regardless of "best practice" defaults.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vestibular sensitivity varies 5–10x across population. Genetic factors, prior motion-sickness history, and medication all shift the comfort baseline. No single design works for all.

failure-mode.description: Hardware drift: principles tuned for Quest 2 fail on Vision Pro (eye-tracking, EyeSight) and vice versa.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each platform has different latency, refresh rate, field of view, and hand-tracking fidelity. Retest on every platform target.
